description_long: This object consists of a pair of traditional Asian-style lanterns, each with a wooden frame and translucent panels featuring delicate floral artwork, likely orchids or bamboo. The lanterns are rectangular with gently curved tops and are supported by four wooden legs. Between the lanterns is a large vertical wooden plaque with prominent white kanji characters, topped by a circular white ring, suggesting a ceremonial or decorative function. In front of the plaque is a small, round, shallow dish with a yin-yang motif, possibly for incense or offerings. The overall arrangement evokes a tranquil, culturally rich ambiance suitable for parks, gardens, or urban plazas.
description: A pair of wooden Asian-style lanterns with floral panels flank a large kanji-inscribed plaque and a yin-yang dish, creating a decorative and ceremonial ensemble.
description_view_0: Front view showing both lanterns, the kanji plaque with its white ring, and the yin-yang dish clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden plaque and the profile of the lanterns; the yin-yang dish is partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back of the plaque and the backs of the lanterns, with no kanji or dish details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the plaque's thickness and lantern profiles from the other side.
